278 HISX - History Education Abroad

HISX205 Rome: Republic and Empire (3 Credits)

Explores the Roman Republic and Empire, from the development of Italic culture in the Bronze Age through the dissolution of the empire in late antiquity. Via primary sources, students examine the development of political forms and ideas in the Mediterranean and their impact on Rome, the relationship of art and literature to society and politics, and developments in the areas of religion, science, and the economy. Credit Only Granted for: HISX205 or HIST269V.
